author: SlightlyConfusedBrit
post:
I moved over to the other side of the world and anxiety about my loved ones is crippling. I'm not diagnosed with any anxiety disorder but over the last year or so I've developed a really intense and frightening paranoia about my loved ones dying/bad things happening to them. I've also been highly paranoid and hyperchondriac of my own health, constantly thinking I will get sick or have some sort of accident. It's pretty bad to the point where I have this ridiculous superstition that I have to 'touch wood' everytime I have one of these thoughts to ensure it won't happen.

A few years ago I moved to the other side of the world away from my family, I fly back to see them around once a year and miss them a lot, despite me being happy with my life overseas. I didn't experience any of this paranoia until the last year or so. I'm with them at the moment so it does feel like the anxiety has accelerated by being in their company, and it's really upsetting me that I can't just enjoy my time without these horrible thoughts. Its bringing me down and making me feel very distressed and anxious to the point where I can't sleep sometimes. I don't want to have these thoughts all the time. I don't really know what's best to do, I used to be so relaxed and chilled out and would love to restore myself back to my carefree self!

author: King-ofHearts
post:
Life is a game of chance, not of luck Is it really so terrible to want something in life? To have the knowledge that everything is going to be okay. I don't. Life doesn't teach us this, life teaches us struggle almost every day to either physically, mentally or emotionally get through each and every day until we run out.

Let's be clear, I'm not talking about money, I'm not talking about job security, I'm not even talking about making sure you're safe. I'm talking about you. You ability to see things how they are and to stop overseeing how things might be. We spend every day thinking to ourselves how it would be better if our lives had turned out a different way, with different people in it and with different days. This is not the way things should be.

Life is a test, and a cruel one at that. Whoever tells you that we fall so we can learn to get ourselves up clearly hasn't fallen hard enough. There comes a point where the fight to get back up is a lot harder than the fall itself, so we learn not to climb too high to avoid the risk all together. Say what you want but this is the general way of the world, the "safe" option, when in reality for some people this isn't safe at all. It's survival.

The happiest man alive could have everything he ever wanted, could have done everything he ever wanted to do and could be loved and respected by everyone around him. This man would still no pain, sorrow, betrayal and rage, so wtf is the point?

author: notreallymetoday1
post:
So tired. Throwaway account.

I've been married for 11 years, I thought our marriage was solid. This past summer, someone who was supposed to be my friend tried to get involved in my relationship, and it nearly broke us. We're trying to fix things, but in the meantime my ex-friend has been slowly taking everything else in my life. She's forged a friendship with my best friend, who has opted to start including her in things and excluding me. She's become BFFs with my mother-in-law, being the daughter-in-law that I haven't been. She has continued eroding my relationship with my husband, telling him things like I'm abusive and I don't treat him fairly. And he's told me that he still cares about her, too.

I was trying to go into 2020 with a good outlook, that things were going to get better, we've been to therapy together, truying to get things back on track. But I found out that my best friend, despite weeks of me trying to plan NYE with her, opted to spend NYE with this ex-friend. I've done NYE with my best friend for like the last 10 years, the lack of plans were hurtful enough, but finding out she made plans with this other undermining person instead of me, despite my asking, hurts more than I can bear.

I feel like I'm not worth anything, that there's no point in trying anymore. She's winning, and I don't know how to fight back. I'm so tired. I'm so tired of hurting and being hurt. She's slowly taking everything I love, winning over my friends and family by pointing out my weaknesses and claiming I'm abusive and cruel. I don't know how to rise above that when I feel totally worthless and wondering if she's right, if I really am a horrible person who doesn't deserve to be loved.

I just feel like it'd be easier to end it all. I can't keep going like this.

author: _I-Hate-Reddit_
post:
Without a stable living situation and losing hope Just over a week ago I left a really shitty situation with parents who have treated me pretty awfully for years and I am finally free - however, I'm homeless. I have been staying with friends and I spend some time in crisis accommodation provided by my psychologist after I finally opened up to her about everything they have been doing to me, and it's just been really hard.

I'm having trouble getting the legal documents I need to secure a stable living situation, in my country so much of that is reliant on parental confirmation even though I am a legal adult, and I met up with my mum today to try and talk things out with her but I got brutalised and put down and just made to feel really worthless all over again. She couldn't even not be cruel to me after meeting for just one day after not seeing eachother for awhile, so I have decided to go no contact.

I feel really helpless. I feel like the systems in place quite literally fuck you over if you have abusive families, needing their birth certificates to verify things. I feel like it has been made near impossible for me to live independently without them but I know I can never go back because they have put me through so much trauma and so many things I know to not be okay and they have no remorse, and I'm never going to put up with that ever again.

I really don't want to go on anymore, I feel so weak, I have been trying so hard to get everything in order for 10 days straight and it has amounted to nothing, and I really feel like I have no other choice,
